## PHASE 0 — DESIGN PROPOSAL (for Anthropic) ### 1) SCOPE CONFIG - **Scope name:** Anthropic (Internal) - **Description:**

Internal hub for Anthropic teams: governance, product updates, safety policy, and operational knowledge—aligned to our mission.

- **Welcome message:**

Welcome to Anthropic’s internal hub. Use it to keep pace with Claude releases, governance decisions, and the policies that make our safety work operational.

- **Scope hero image:** (Brand logo upload happens later) - **Logo.dev brand logo URL (for Phase 1.5):** https://img.logo.dev/anthropic.com?token=pk_OwST1E04QH-w3oM0YksM2Q&retina=true - **Brand colors (from profile, including inferred warn):** - **primary_color:** #D4A27F - **primary_color_contrast:** #191919 - **secondary_color:** #191919 - **warn_color:** #C0392B - **warn_color_contrast:** #FFFFFF - **Highlights config:** - **number_of_highlights:** 4 - **highlights_carousel:** true - **highlights_style:** BannerCarousel - **Footer (3 columns with real links from profile):** 1. **About** - Careers — https://www.anthropic.com/careers - Research — https://www.anthropic.com/research - Newsroom — https://www.anthropic.com/news 2. **Responsible AI** - Responsible Scaling Policy — https://www.anthropic.com/responsible-scaling-policy - Safety — https://www.anthropic.com/safety - Long-Term Benefit Trust — https://www.anthropic.com/news/the-long-term-benefit-trust 3. **Developer** - Developer docs — https://docs.anthropic.com - Claude.ai — https://claude.ai - LinkedIn (company) — https://www.linkedin.com/company/anthropicresearch - **Social links (from profile):** - LinkedIn — https://www.linkedin.com/company/anthropicresearch - Twitter / X — https://twitter.com/AnthropicAI - YouTube — https://www.youtube.com/@anthropic-ai - **navigation_links:** - **topBarLinks (3):** - Research — https://www.anthropic.com/research - Responsible Scaling Policy — https://www.anthropic.com/responsible-scaling-policy - Developer docs — https://docs.anthropic.com - **footerLinks (5):** - Careers — https://www.anthropic.com/careers - Newsroom — https://www.anthropic.com/news - Safety — https://www.anthropic.com/safety - Long-Term Benefit Trust — https://www.anthropic.com/news/the-long-term-benefit-trust - Developer docs — https://docs.anthropic.com - **components (pick 3):** - **recentDocuments** (knowledge-heavy org; ties to research/docs workflows) - **upcomingEvents** (town halls + product/launch events) - **workAnniversaries** (culture reinforcement) --- ### 2) CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5 channels) **Color + image assignment:** - Primary channels use Anthropic tan/dark; secondary channels are utility-coded with warn/tan or neutral cool. - **Channel image_url** values come strictly from provided stock images list (or null). #### Ch1 — “Claude & Product Deployment Briefing” (PRIMARY, sticky) - **Purpose:** Single place for what changed in Claude models, Claude Code, and agentic deployments—and what teams must do next. - **Type:** public - **Color:** #D4A27F - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** stock/Innovation.png - **FAQ (2-3):** 1. **Q: Where do I find what’s new in Claude Code integrations?** **A:** This channel posts integration and agentic workflow updates after major milestones (e.g., Claude Code GA in May 2025; subsequent capability updates). Start with the most recent “Deployment Briefing” post and follow the linked runbook items. 2. **Q: Are model changes gated by safety policy?** **A:** Yes. Deployment notes here always reference the relevant safety gating context (e.g., ASL-3 activation in May 2025 alongside Claude 4) so teams know what changed and why. 3. **Q: Who should I contact if an update breaks a workflow?** **A:** Use the “Report an integration issue” CTA in each post; escalations are routed to the owning integration group. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description (HTML, 2-3 sentences):**

Claude releases move fast; your operating context shouldn’t. This briefing channel consolidates the model/product timeline (Claude families, Claude Code, Claude Cowork) with deployment-oriented next steps.

We aim for evidence-forward updates—what changed, what we tested, and what teams should do now.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' #### Ch2 — “Safety Gates & Responsible Scaling” (PRIMARY, sticky) - **Purpose:** Responsible Scaling Policy, ASL gates, and safety-critical implementation requirements for teams shipping models and tooling. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** #C0392B - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** stock/security.png - **FAQ:** 1. **Q: What changed with ASL-3 safeguards activation in May 2025?** **A:** This channel explains the operational meaning of the gate (what must be true before deployment) and what evidence is expected for sign-off. 2. **Q: Where do I find the “Responsible Scaling Policy” for internal use?** **A:** Start with the policy acknowledgement post in this channel. It includes the most current link set and what “acknowledge” means for your team. 3. **Q: What if I think a safety gate is overly blocking?** **A:** Raise it through the documented exception path referenced in the post; the goal is clarity and accountability, not bypasses. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description:**

Anthropic’s RSP isn’t a principle doc—it’s a deployment gate. This channel captures how safety requirements translate into concrete readiness criteria for model and tooling release.

Every policy-critical item here requires explicit read confirmation.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' #### Ch3 — “People, Growth & Mission-Primacy” (PRIMARY, sticky) - **Purpose:** Workforce updates, onboarding, culture reinforcement, and practical guides for scaling without losing the mission. - **Type:** public - **Color:** #D4A27F - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** stock/community.png - **FAQ:** 1. **Q: What should I expect during rapid growth (career ladders, processes)?** **A:** We acknowledge growing pains: role definitions and ladders may lag. This channel posts “how we work now” updates and where to send questions. 2. **Q: How do we handle feedback on unclear promotion criteria?** **A:** Use the “Scaling playbook” guide; it describes the feedback loop and where calibration updates appear. 3. **Q: What’s the clearest cultural signal of mission primacy?** **A:** Written and operational decisions: who participates in research discussions, how safety gating is handled, and how teams prioritize long-term benefit over short-term optics. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description:**

Anthropic’s culture is embedded in how teams operate: high-trust collaboration, mission primacy, and evidence-based decisions. This channel makes those expectations concrete as we scale.

Find onboarding guidance, benefits/program updates, and leadership notes that translate intent into day-to-day practice.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' #### Ch4 — “Governance, Trust & Long-Term Benefit” (SECONDARY) - **Purpose:** Long-Term Benefit Trust updates and governance context so teams understand why board outcomes matter for mission integrity. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** #191919 - **is_sticky:** False - **image_url:** stock/Decision.png - **FAQ (policy-adjacent but not redundant):** 1. **Q: What is the Long-Term Benefit Trust and why does it constrain investor control?** **A:** It’s a purpose trust designed to prevent mission drift under commercial pressure, with trustees holding power to elect board members over time. 2. **Q: Where do I learn about LTBT trustees and board-majority changes?** **A:** This channel posts governance updates with the relevant timeline and what employees should take away. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** True - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description:**

Governance decisions can feel abstract—until they protect the mission in practice. This channel translates LTBT structure and trustee milestones into team-level context.

Use it when you need the “why” behind constraints we operate under.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' #### Ch5 — “Security, Privacy & Systems Hygiene” (SECONDARY) - **Purpose:** Security policies, account safety, and practical guidance for keeping systems, data, and access controls robust. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** #C0392B - **is_sticky:** False - **image_url:** stock/Password.png - **FAQ:** 1. **Q: What are the security expectations for handling sensitive model/deployment info?** **A:** Follow the required security guide; it covers access control discipline, incident reporting, and least-privilege expectations. 2. **Q: Where is the current password and access hygiene checklist?** **A:** The checklist post includes the latest operational checklist and acknowledgement instructions. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** True - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description:**

Security is a daily practice, not a one-time checklist. This channel provides the operational expectations for accounts, data handling, and incident readiness.

Security-critical items require read confirmation.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' --- ### 3) CONTENT CALENDAR (all Published; 1-2 Events total) **Homepage highlights rule:** exactly **3-4 items total** with **is_main_highlight=True**. I’m setting **4** highlights across the hub (measured, high-signal): funding/revenue, Claude Code revenue, ASL-3 gate, and a major governance update. #### Ch1 — Claude & Product Deployment Briefing (3 items; sticky on 1-2) 1) **Title:** Claude Opus 4 / Sonnet 4 Deployment Notes: Hybrid Reasoning Update - **Type:** spotlight - **tags:** ['claude-4', 'deployment', 'reasoning', 'hybrid-modes'] - **is_main_highlight:** True - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/DataMaintenance.png - **Body outline:** Explain hybrid reasoning modes, what changed operationally since May 2025, and how teams should validate long-horizon tasks and coding workflows. - **CTA line:** “If your workflow depends on reasoning depth, add your test results to the linked thread.” 2) **Title:** Claude Code Reaches $2.5B+ Annualised Revenue — What’s Driving Adoption - **Type:** milestone - **tags:** ['claude-code', 'revenue', 'agents', 'enterprise'] - **is_main_highlight:** True - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** stock/financial_report.png - **Body outline:** Summarize the Feb–May 2026 adoption trajectory (e.g., exceeded $2.5B annualised revenue by Feb 2026; 33% of revenue by May 2026), describe why agentic multi-step actions matter, and list practical deployment learnings for enterprise teams. - **CTA line:** “Share your team’s top three ‘agentic wins’ so we can prioritize integration hardening.” 3) **Title:** Claude Cowork Rollout Brief: From Research Preview to Enterprise Workflows - **Type:** recap - **tags:** ['claude-cowork', 'workflows', 'enterprise', 'desktop-agent'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/Message.png - **Body outline:** Capture the early 2026 launch premise (desktop-based agent) and concrete outcomes from the PwC partnership example (underwriting cycles 10 weeks → 10 days), plus rollout readiness check guidance. - **CTA line:** “Use the onboarding checklist in this post before enabling Cowork for new business units.” --- #### Ch2 — Safety Gates & Responsible Scaling (3 items; policy acknowledgements required) 1) **Title:** ASL-3 Safeguards Activation (May 2025): Operational Meaning for Release Readiness - **Type:** policy - **tags:** ['rsp', 'asl-3', 'safety-gates', 'deployment'] - **is_main_highlight:** True - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** True - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** stock/audit.png - **Body outline:** Describe what ASL-3 activation represents as a deployment gate, explain what teams must verify, and list evidence expectations and where to record sign-off. - **CTA line:** “Acknowledge this post to confirm you’re aligning release workflows with the RSP gate.” 2) **Title:** Responsible Scaling Policy v3.1 (RSP 3.1): How Teams Interpret “Adequate Safeguards” - **Type:** policy - **tags:** ['responsible-scaling', 'policy-3-1', 'interpretation', 'safety-evidence'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** True - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/risk.png - **Body outline:** Translate RSP 3.1 into practical interpretation guidance (what counts as adequate safeguards), with examples of review artefacts and decision traceability expectations. - **CTA line:** “If you’re unsure whether an evidence package qualifies, request a pre-merge review.” 3) **Title:** Safety Evaluation Methods for Claude 4: What We Tested Before Release - **Type:** guide - **tags:** ['evaluation', 'claude-4', 'testing', 'safety-methods'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/QualityCheck.png - **Body outline:** Summarize evaluation framing around release readiness (still evidence-first; no hype), and how teams should report findings and uncertainties. - **CTA line:** “Download the evaluation template link and attach results to your release brief.” --- #### Ch3 — People, Growth & Mission-Primacy (3 items; include onboarding and culture) 1) **Title:** Scaling Without Losing Mission Primacy: How We Keep High-Trust Collaboration Real - **Type:** guide - **tags:** ['mission-primacy', 'culture', 'high-trust', 'scaling'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/community.png - **Body outline:** Describe the small-team autonomy model (2–4 people), explain what changes during rapid growth, and provide practical habits for low-ego collaboration and mission-first decision-making. - **CTA line:** “Post your team’s ‘works in practice’ rituals so others can adapt them.” 2) **Title:** Benefits & Work Model Checkpoint: Wellness Stipends, Meals, and Hybrid Expectations - **Type:** guide - **tags:** ['benefits', 'hybrid', 'pto', 'wellness'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/onboarding.png - **Body outline:** Provide a consolidated guide to benefits highlights (wellness stipends, employer-paid meals, commuter support, home setup assistance, relocation support) and explain hybrid expectations (roughly 25% in-office time, SF as center of gravity). - **CTA line:** “If your role location plan is unclear, ask in #people-ops with your team context.” 3) **Title:** 2026 Growth Rhythm: What “Triple the Workforce” Means for Processes - **Type:** recap - **tags:** ['growth', '2026', 'process', 'headcount'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** stock/team_diversity.png - **Body outline:** A realistic acknowledgement of scaling pains (career ladders/promotion criteria lag), what’s changing, and where to find updates; include factual anchors like growth trajectory from early 2023 to early 2026. - **CTA line:** “Bring your friction points—this channel will turn them into process improvements.” --- #### Ch4 — Governance, Trust & Long-Term Benefit (2 items) 1) **Title:** Long-Term Benefit Trust Milestone: Board-Majority Context (as of April 2026) - **Type:** policy - **tags:** ['ltbt', 'governance', 'board', 'mission-integrity'] - **is_main_highlight:** True - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** True - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** stock/Decision.png - **Body outline:** Explain LTBT purpose trust mechanics (Class T Common Stock power, trustees disinterested, increasing board influence) and reference trustee names and April 2026 majority context; add what employees should understand about mission protection. - **CTA line:** “Please acknowledge so you can align stakeholder conversations with governance reality.” 2) **Title:** LTBT Trustee Update: Why Governance Decisions Are Operational Decisions - **Type:** recap - **tags:** ['trustees', 'governance-why', 'mission', 'context'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** True - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/leadership_speech.png - **Body outline:** Tie trustee milestone significance to concrete constraints teams operate under; include named context from the profile and a practical FAQ for employees. - **CTA line:** “If you need the 2-minute version for a stakeholder meeting, reference the FAQ section above.” --- #### Ch5 — Security, Privacy & Systems Hygiene (2 items; both read-confirmation) 1) **Title:** Password & Access Hygiene Checklist (Security Baseline for Growth in 2026) - **Type:** policy - **tags:** ['security', 'access', 'passwords', 'hygiene'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** True - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** stock/Password.png - **Body outline:** Provide operational security baseline expectations (account discipline, least privilege reminder, incident readiness), and explain how the checklist supports safe scaling. - **CTA line:** “Acknowledge after you’ve applied the checklist to your systems.” 2) **Title:** Incident Response: How to Report, Triage, and Close Security Events - **Type:** guide - **tags:** ['incident-response', 'security', 'triage', 'comms'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** True - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/security.png - **Body outline:** Give a practical runbook for reporting, what “good reports” contain, and how closure communication should work; include expectations for evidence and timelines (no fabrication). - **CTA line:** “When in doubt, report early—precision comes from iteration.” --- #### Events (1–2 total across the hub) - **Event 1 in Ch1 (town hall / launch-style):** - **Channel:** Ch1 - **Title:** Claude Product & Deployment Town Hall: From Hybrid Reasoning to Agentic Workflows - **Type:** event - **tags:** ['town-hall', 'claude-code', 'claude-cowork', 'deployment'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** Published - **notify:** True - **image_url:** stock/video_call.png - **event_start:** 2026-07-15T17:00:00Z - **event_end:** 2026-07-15T18:00:00Z - **location:** San Francisco + virtual (hybrid) *(location phrased without inventing a specific venue address)* - **Body outline:** Agenda anchored to the profile’s Claude Code/Cowork growth and deployment lessons; include Q&A format and how teams should submit questions in advance. - **CTA line:** “Submit questions by end of day two days before the session.” - **Event 2 (optional but recommended) in Ch2 or Ch4 (safety/legal-aligned):** - **Channel:** Ch2 - **Title:** RSP 3.1 Readout & Release Gate Office Hours (ASL-3) - **Type:** event - **tags:** ['rsp', 'asl-3', 'office-hours', 'safety'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** True *(attendees asked to acknowledge beforehand via the policy post)* - **status:** Published - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/reading.png - **event_start:** 2026-08-05T18:00:00Z - **event_end:** 2026-08-05T19:00:00Z - **location:** Virtual (global teams) - **Body outline:** Focused office hours on interpreting “adequate safeguards,” plus how to package evidence for review; clarify what counts and what doesn’t. - **CTA line:** “Bring one release example where you want a safety-gate interpretation check.” --- ### 4) HUB-LEVEL POLISH (for Phase 4) - **company_name:** Anthropic PBC (Public Benefit Corporation) - **website_name:** Anthropic - **default_language:** en-US - **supported_languages:** ['en-US'] *(based on profile HQ in US; other offices exist but profile doesn’t specify language tags—if you want broader, tell me which locales to include)* - **login_quick_links (from profile, 5 URLs):** - Careers — https://www.anthropic.com/careers - Newsroom — https://www.anthropic.com/news - Responsible Scaling Policy — https://www.anthropic.com/responsible-scaling-policy - Developer docs — https://docs.anthropic.com - Claude.ai — https://claude.ai - **sidebar_navigation (ordered priorities):** 1. **collaborators** (high-trust collaboration, small autonomous teams, culture) 2. **documents** (policies, safety gates, security guidance; plus research-style docs) 3. **workgroups** (people + growth + onboarding operations; reflects scaling rhythms) 4. **learning** (guides, evaluation methods, office hours) 5. **approvalRequests** (policy acknowledgements / governance acknowledgement flow) 6. **apps** (Claude/Claude Code/Claude Cowork as “apps” entry point) 7. **ideation** (optional: product/innovation updates flow; included last) --- ## Confirmation (IMPORTANT) This is a complete design. Shall I build it end-to-end, or would you like to adjust anything first — channel names, content plan, colors, or hub settings?

arguments.name: Anthropic (Internal)
arguments.description: Internal hub for Anthropic teams: governance, product updates, safety policy, and operational knowledge—aligned to our mission.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome to Anthropic’s internal hub. Use it to keep pace with Claude releases, governance decisions, and the policies that make our safety work operational.

channels.name: Claude & Product Deployment Briefing
channels.description:
Claude releases move fast; your operating context shouldn’t. This briefing channel consolidates the model/product timeline (Claude families, Claude Code, Claude Cowork) with deployment-oriented next steps.

We aim for evidence-forward updates—what changed, what we tested, and what teams should do now.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find what’s new in Claude Code integrations?
frequent_questions.answer: This channel posts integration and agentic workflow updates after major milestones (e.g., Claude Code GA in May 2025; subsequent capability updates). Start with the most recent “Deployment Briefing” post and follow the linked runbook items.

frequent_questions.question: Are model changes gated by safety policy?
frequent_questions.answer: Yes. Deployment notes here always reference the relevant safety gating context (e.g., ASL-3 activation in May 2025 alongside Claude 4) so teams know what changed and why.

frequent_questions.question: Who should I contact if an update breaks a workflow?
frequent_questions.answer: Use the “Report an integration issue” CTA in each post; escalations are routed to the owning integration group.

channels.name: Safety Gates & Responsible Scaling
channels.description:
Anthropic’s RSP isn’t a principle doc—it’s a deployment gate. This channel captures how safety requirements translate into concrete readiness criteria for model and tooling release.

Every policy-critical item here requires explicit read confirmation.

frequent_questions.question: What changed with ASL-3 safeguards activation in May 2025?
frequent_questions.answer: This channel explains the operational meaning of the gate (what must be true before deployment) and what evidence is expected for sign-off.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find the “Responsible Scaling Policy” for internal use?
frequent_questions.answer: Start with the policy acknowledgement post in this channel. It includes the most current link set and what “acknowledge” means for your team.

frequent_questions.question: What if I think a safety gate is overly blocking?
frequent_questions.answer: Raise it through the documented exception path referenced in the post; the goal is clarity and accountability, not bypasses.

channels.name: People, Growth & Mission-Primacy
channels.description:
Anthropic’s culture is embedded in how teams operate: high-trust collaboration, mission primacy, and evidence-based decisions. This channel makes those expectations concrete as we scale.

Find onboarding guidance, benefits/program updates, and leadership notes that translate intent into day-to-day practice.

frequent_questions.question: What should I expect during rapid growth (career ladders, processes)?
frequent_questions.answer: We acknowledge growing pains: role definitions and ladders may lag. This channel posts “how we work now” updates and where to send questions.

frequent_questions.question: How do we handle feedback on unclear promotion criteria?
frequent_questions.answer: Use the “Scaling playbook” guide; it describes the feedback loop and where calibration updates appear.

frequent_questions.question: What’s the clearest cultural signal of mission primacy?
frequent_questions.answer: Written and operational decisions: who participates in research discussions, how safety gating is handled, and how teams prioritize long-term benefit over short-term optics.

channels.name: Governance, Trust & Long-Term Benefit
channels.description:
Governance decisions can feel abstract—until they protect the mission in practice. This channel translates LTBT structure and trustee milestones into team-level context.

Use it when you need the “why” behind constraints we operate under.

frequent_questions.question: What is the Long-Term Benefit Trust and why does it constrain investor control?
frequent_questions.answer: It’s a purpose trust designed to prevent mission drift under commercial pressure, with trustees holding power to elect board members over time.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I learn about LTBT trustees and board-majority changes?
frequent_questions.answer: This channel posts governance updates with the relevant timeline and what employees should take away.

channels.name: Security, Privacy & Systems Hygiene
channels.description:
Security is a daily practice, not a one-time checklist. This channel provides the operational expectations for accounts, data handling, and incident readiness.

Security-critical items require read confirmation.

frequent_questions.question: What are the security expectations for handling sensitive model/deployment info?
frequent_questions.answer: Follow the required security guide; it covers access control discipline, incident reporting, and least-privilege expectations.

frequent_questions.question: Where is the current password and access hygiene checklist?
frequent_questions.answer: The checklist post includes the latest operational checklist and acknowledgement instructions.
